item.title: Best gold IRA companies and investing advice to know now
item.description: Want to protect your portfolio from rising inflation? These top gold IRA companies can help you invest smarter now.

item.title: Democratic lawmakers slam Trump for not signing housing act
item.description: Democratic congressional leaders spoke Wednesday after President Trump canceled a signing ceremony for a landmark housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins. Trump said he will not sign the legislation until the SAVE America Act is passed.

item.title: How much interest can a $45,000 CD account earn now?
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account will be significant and accessible in just a few months. Here's what to know now.

item.title: Trump meets with GOP senators after refusing to sign housing bill
item.description: President Trump's decision to abruptly cancel the signing of a landmark bipartisan housing bill marked the latest misalignment between him and GOP lawmakers.

item.title: Trump to ask Congress to make 15% ethanol-gas blend permanent
item.description: E15 is usually only available part of the year to help ease high gas prices under a waiver from the EPA.

item.title: Best debt relief companies, plus advice borrowers need to know now
item.description: These debt relief companies could help you slash your debt, but there are some things to know before signing up.

item.title: Will mortgage interest rates drop in July? 4 things to consider now
item.description: Today's mortgage interest rates don't necessarily have to remain high this July. Here's what to consider right now.

item.title: Chemours to pay $450M in first federal "forever chemicals" settlement
item.description: Chemical maker Chemours allegedly discharged chemicals linked to cancer and other health conditions in three states.

item.title: TV host sorry for slamming player for leaving World Cup for baby's birth
item.description: France Pierron described childbirth as "a disgusting moment, excuse me, where the dad is useless" during a TV appearance.

item.title: Trump cancels housing bill signing, reiterates demand for SAVE America Act
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.

item.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
item.description: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

item.title: Sneak peek: The Day My Mother Never Came Home
item.description: ENCORE: Did a father use his 6-year-old son as an alibi for murder? A son grapples with his parents' troubled past. "48 Hours" contributor Vladimir Duthiers reports Saturday, June 27 at 10/9c* on CBS and streaming on Paramount+. (Following the WNBA on CBS).

item.title: How much interest would a $10,000 CD earn in 3 years?
item.description: Returns on a $10,000 3-year CD account may be substantial, but that's not the only benefit for savers who act now.

item.title: Mamdani says progressive primary victories show hunger "for a new kind of politics"
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said the progressive message to the average person is driving wins in Democratic primaries in his state. On Capitol Hill, House Speaker Mike Johnson shared his reaction.

item.title: FBI, NYPD carry out searches tied to bribery investigation of police officials
item.description: The searches stemmed from an ongoing probe into the conduct of former NYPD Chief of Department Jeffrey Maddrey, a source told CBS News.

item.title: Ancient Egyptian burial site reveals centuries of changing traditions
item.description: Human and animal remains unearthed in Egypt's Nile Delta reveal changing funerary practices over some 600 years, and the evolution of a key site itself.

item.title: Which debts are surviving family members responsible when someone dies?
item.description: Some types of debt may be wiped out, but a few obligations can still follow your surviving family members.

item.title: Bill Gates said Epstein tried to use knowledge of his affairs "to pressure me"
item.description: Bill Gates testified June 10 for nearly six hours before the House Oversight Committee, which is examining the government's handling of the Epstein case and those with ties to him.

item.title: Rubio renews threats that Trump will take other action if Iran doesn't comply in talks
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters after meetings with allies in Kuwait, saying President Trump could still take action if Iran doesn't reach a deal with the U.S. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: Trump-linked firm is lobbying for pardons and its first client already paid $500K
item.description: Mo Strategies, started by former Trump campaign and administration officials, recently expanded its practice into the lucrative world of pardon lobbying.

item.title: Trump-linked firm is lobbying for pardons, CBS News investigation shows
item.description: Mo Strategies, a firm linked to President Trump, is lobbying for pardons, according to a CBS News investigation. CBS News' Gabe Kaminsky has more.

item.title: Eric Adams' former chief of staff arrested in federal bribery probe, sources say
item.description: Frank Carone, the former chief of staff to former New York City Mayor Eric Adams, was arrested as part of a federal bribery probe, sources say. Meanwhile, the FBI and NYPD are investigating former NYPD officials for bribery. CBS News' Anna Shecter has more.

item.title: Zuckerberg could be betting on new prediction market app, reports say
item.description: Mike Isaac, a tech reporter for The New York Times, joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss a story he helped break about Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg directing the company to develop a prediction market app.

item.title: Trump cancels bill signing for bipartisan housing act, demands passage of SAVE America Act first
item.description: President Trump canceled the signing of the bipartisan housing bill, saying he won't sign it until the SAVE America Act passes. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Who were the top NBA Draft first round picks?
item.description: AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer were the standout picks during the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft. Sports Illustrated's Derek Parker breaks down the draft selections.

item.title: Latino voters could be key to midterms. What's driving them?
item.description: Leading Latino political strategists are examining the races where Latinos could play an outsize role in the midterm elections, and believe their votes will be critical in a number of House races.

item.title: Ex-Oklahoma inmate who was nearly executed 3 times gets new trial
item.description: A new murder trial is scheduled for Richard Glossip, a former Oklahoma death row inmate who was released on bond last month after being on the brink of execution three times.

item.title: Wounded soldiers, families accuse Army of downplaying war injuries
item.description: Soldiers say the Army disregarded warnings about thin defenses and ignored requests for medical supplies. Now they question whether the Army is being transparent about their injuries.

item.title: Mamdani influences New York City's congressional primaries with 3 big wins
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three left-of-center candidates in the congressional Democratic primaries, and all three are set to win, CBS News projects.

item.title: Extended interview: "Real Housewives" star Dorinda Medley
item.description: Dorinda Medley, who appeared in the "Real Housewives of New York City" for six seasons, talks to CBS Mornings' Vladimir Duthiers about her decision to join the Bravo series, why she has no regrets and what draws people to reality TV.
